Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function, likely acute or chronic kidney disease with possible nephrotic features. The presence of glucosuria and ketonuria may indicate poor glycemic control or possible diabetes. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
